The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the management of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Peyritschia conferta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
